title: beta vulgaris var. maritima
intro: Beta vulgaris var. maritima, also known as sea beet, is a wild form of the beetroot plant that grows in coastal areas. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral effects, due to the presence of betalains and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Sea beet can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
